meta.title: SAP FICO Interview Questions | Teks Academy
meta.description: Explore top SAP FICO interview questions and answers covering Financial Accounting, Controlling, SAP ERP, Asset Accounting, and Financial Reporting.

heading: SAP FICO
Interview Questions
heroSection.subHeading: Prepare for SAP FICO interviews with finance and ERP-focused technical questions and answers.

questions.question: What is SAP FICO?
questions.answer: SAP FICO stands for Financial Accounting (FI) and Controlling (CO). It is a core module in SAP for financial reporting and internal management.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the FI module in SAP?
questions.answer: To record financial transactions in real time and provide accurate financial statements.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the CO module?
questions.answer: It supports internal reporting, cost control, and performance monitoring by tracking and managing costs.

questions.question: What are the organizational units in SAP FI?
questions.answer: Company Code, Business Area, Chart of Accounts, Functional Area.

questions.question: What is a Company Code?
questions.answer: A unique identifier for an independent accounting unit. Financial statements are created at this level.

questions.question: What is a Chart of Accounts?
questions.answer: A list of all general ledger (G/L) accounts used by one or more company codes.

questions.question: What are the types of Chart of Accounts?
questions.answer: Operating, Group, and Country-specific Chart of Accounts.

questions.question: What is a G/L Account?
questions.answer: A ledger to record all accounting transactions.

questions.question: What is a fiscal year variant?
questions.answer: It defines the financial year’s structure — how months and periods are distributed.

questions.question: What is a posting period?
questions.answer: Time periods in which transactions can be posted. Usually defined as months.

questions.question: How many normal and special periods can be defined?
questions.answer: 12 normal periods and 4 special periods.

questions.question: What is a field status group?
questions.answer: It controls the fields during document entry (optional, mandatory, suppressed).

questions.question: What is a document type?
questions.answer: Classification of accounting documents such as vendor invoice (KR), customer invoice (DR), etc.

questions.question: What is posting key?
questions.answer: Determines the type of account (customer/vendor/G/L) and debit/credit indicator.

questions.question: What are retained earnings accounts?
questions.answer: Accounts used to carry forward balances from one fiscal year to the next.

questions.question: What is an account group?
questions.answer: It controls the number range and field status of G/L, customer, or vendor accounts.

questions.question: What is a reconciliation account?
questions.answer: A G/L account used to consolidate sub-ledger (AR/AP/Assets) balances.

questions.question: What is open item management?
questions.answer: Ensures that transactions like invoices and payments are matched.

questions.question: What is a dunning process?
questions.answer: An automated reminder system for overdue customer invoices.

questions.question: What is a payment term?
questions.answer: Terms defined for invoice payments (e.g., net 30, 2% 10/net 30).

questions.question: What is automatic payment program (APP)?
questions.answer: A tool to automate vendor/customer payments based on configuration.

questions.question: What is asset accounting in SAP?
questions.answer: Sub-module of FI to manage and monitor fixed assets.

questions.question: What are asset classes?
questions.answer: Categories used to group assets with similar characteristics.

questions.question: What is depreciation area?
questions.answer: It represents different valuation types (e.g., book depreciation, tax depreciation).

questions.question: What is WBS in SAP CO?
questions.answer: Work Breakdown Structure – used in project systems for planning and tracking.

questions.question: What is a cost center?
questions.answer: An organizational unit that collects costs related to a business function.

questions.question: What is an internal order?
questions.answer: A tool for tracking costs of specific tasks, projects, or events.

questions.question: What is a profit center?
questions.answer: A part of an organization for which profit and loss is calculated.

questions.question: What is a controlling area?
questions.answer: The organizational unit in CO that represents a closed system for cost accounting.

questions.question: What is a cost element?
questions.answer: It identifies the type of cost incurred in CO (similar to G/L accounts in FI).

questions.question: What is a primary cost element?
questions.answer: Costs directly from FI like salaries, rent, etc.

questions.question: What is a secondary cost element?
questions.answer: Costs used only in CO for internal allocations (like assessments, activity allocations).

questions.question: What is an activity type?
questions.answer: Used to allocate costs internally based on services provided (e.g., machine hours).

questions.question: What is settlement in SAP CO?
questions.answer: Transferring costs from one object (e.g., internal order) to another (e.g., cost center).

questions.question: What is real vs statistical posting?
questions.answer: Real postings impact balances; statistical postings are for reporting purposes only.

questions.question: What is parallel accounting?
questions.answer: Managing multiple accounting principles (e.g., IFRS and local GAAP) using different ledgers.

questions.question: What are validations and substitutions?
questions.answer: Used to enforce business rules during document posting.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the closing cockpit?
questions.answer: Automates and monitors period-end closing activities.

questions.question: What is document splitting?
questions.answer: Splits accounting entries to enable detailed reporting (e.g., by segment or business area).

questions.question: What is a ledger in SAP S/4HANA?
questions.answer: Represents accounting principles; multiple ledgers allow parallel reporting.

questions.question: What is universal journal (ACDOCA)?
questions.answer: A single table that holds all accounting documents in S/4HANA, simplifying reporting.

questions.question: What is CO-PA (Profitability Analysis)?
questions.answer: A tool in CO to analyze the profitability of business segments.

questions.question: What is the difference between costing-based and account-based CO-PA?
questions.answer: Costing-based is flexible with internal values; account-based ties directly to FI postings.

questions.question: What is a special G/L transaction?
questions.answer: Transactions like down payments, bills of exchange – not regular AP/AR postings.

questions.question: What are recurring entries?
questions.answer: Periodic postings like rent, created automatically based on a template.

questions.question: What is SAP S/4HANA Finance?
questions.answer: A simplified finance solution in SAP HANA with innovations like Universal Journal.

questions.question: What is real-time integration in SAP FICO?
questions.answer: Ensures instant and automatic data flow between FI and CO modules.

questions.question: What is the role of SAP Fiori in FICO?
questions.answer: Offers user-friendly apps for financial processes like payments, invoices, and reporting.

questions.question: What is a balance sheet?
questions.answer: A financial statement showing assets, liabilities, and equity at a point in time.

questions.question: What is the trial balance?
questions.answer: A report showing all G/L balances to verify accounting accuracy before financial statements.
